plan_adherence_notes: All 4 plan steps were executed correctly. Step 1 (write lock): handle_market_resolution core logic wrapped in get_market_write_lock(market_id), matching the pattern of other mutation handlers. Step 2 (resolvedAt): market['resolvedAt'] = resolved_at added in transition_market_to_resolved after setting marginals. Step 3 (test): test_market_resolution_sets_resolved_at_on_market_object added with 7 assertions covering field presence, type, Zulu format, ISO parsing, UTC timezone, and market object persistence. Minor gap: plan called for 2 tests (resolvedAt + write lock confirmation), but only 1 test was added (resolvedAt only). The write lock change is implicitly validated by the 25 existing resolution tests all passing, so this is acceptable. Step 4 (run tests): 269 tests pass, 0 failures. Deducting 0.1 for the missing dedicated write-lock test.
missed_questions: Should the resolvedAt field be included in market summary/list responses, or only in the full market object? (minor — the field is on the market dict so it would appear wherever the full market is serialized)
Are there any downstream consumers (e.g. WebSocket broadcasts, analytics) that need to be updated to include resolvedAt? (the ws_broadcast already sends market state, so it may already propagate)
reason: The execution faithfully implemented the plan's 3 core changes: (1) write lock wrapping the entire resolution handler to fix the TOCTOU race condition, (2) resolvedAt timestamp persisted on the market object, (3) a well-structured test validating the new field. All 269 tests pass with 0 failures, including 25 resolution-specific tests. The research correctly identified this as a hardening task on an existing feature, and the plan scoped the work appropriately to ~20 lines of meaningful changes across server.py and the test file. The only minor gap is the absence of a dedicated write-lock test, but the existing test suite provides sufficient implicit coverage since all resolution tests exercise the locked code path.
